changeSet.author: dharani
changeSet.sql: -- Inline normalisation: digits-only 10-char → +91..., 12-char starting 91 → +91..., etc. -- Skip rows already in E.164 (start with +) and rows whose normalised form would collide. UPDATE customers c SET phone = normalized.e164 FROM ( SELECT id, CASE WHEN regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+91' || regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') WHEN regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^91[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+' || regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') WHEN regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^0091[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+91' || substr(regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g'), 5) ELSE phone END AS e164 FROM customers WHERE phone IS NOT NULL AND phone != '' AND phone NOT LIKE '+%' ) AS normalized WHERE c.id = normalized.id -- Skip if (a) another row already holds this E.164 value, OR -- (b) a lower-id row would also normalize to this same E.164 value. -- Condition (b) ensures that among a group of duplicate raw numbers only the -- earliest-registered customer gets normalised; the rest are left as-is and -- must be resolved manually (merged / deactivated) before a future migration. AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM customers other WHERE other.id != c.id AND ( other.phone = normalized.e164 OR ( other.id < c.id AND CASE WHEN regexp_replace(other.phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+91' || regexp_replace(other.phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') WHEN regexp_replace(other.phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^91[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+' || regexp_replace(other.phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') WHEN regexp_replace(other.phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^0091[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+91' || substr(regexp_replace(other.phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g'), 5) ELSE other.phone END = normalized.e164 ) ) );

rollback.sql: -- Best-effort rollback: strip +91 prefix from 13-char E.164 numbers only. -- Cannot recover numbers that were already in non-standard format before the migration. UPDATE customers SET phone = substr(phone, 4) WHERE phone ~ '^\+91[6-9][0-9]{9}$';

changeSet.author: dharani
changeSet.sql: UPDATE customers SET phone = regexp_replace(phone, '\s+', '', 'g') WHERE phone LIKE '+%' AND phone ~ '\s';

changeSet.author: dharani
changeSet.sql: UPDATE customer_whatsapp_preferences SET phone = CASE WHEN regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+91' || regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') WHEN regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^91[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+' || regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') WHEN regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g') ~ '^0091[6-9][0-9]{9}$' THEN '+91' || substr(regexp_replace(phone, '[^0-9]', '', 'g'), 5) ELSE phone END WHERE phone IS NOT NULL AND phone != '' AND phone NOT LIKE '+%';
